vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #1722

plan.title: feat(ts-migration): port policy surface (show / allow-direct-commits / disclosure) to TS (Wave 2)

narratives.Description: Port the typed branch-policy resolution, the policy:show and policy:allow-direct-commits surfaces, and disclosure_line from scripts/policy.py into the @deftai/core TypeScript module. The port preserves byte-identical disclosure phrasing and the audit-row write, and proves parity against the Python oracle via cache-off golden-diff fixtures.
narratives.Overview:
## Parent

#1530 (Part 1 of 2; umbrella epic #1545)

## What to build

Port `scripts/policy.py` (the typed policy resolution, `policy:show`, `policy:allow-direct-commits`, and `disclosure_line`) to TS, preserving the audit-row write and disclosure phrasing.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `policy:show` / `policy:allow-direct-commits` run on TS with identical resolution semantics
- [ ] `disclosure_line` phrasing is byte-identical
- [ ] Golden-output parity vs the Python oracle (cache-off)
- [ ] `task check` passes

## Standing invariants (all Part-1 children)
- The Python suite is the **parity oracle** and runs **wholesale** (not affected-sliced) until deleted.
- `task check` stays green at every step (strangler-fig; no big-bang).
- The parity gate runs **cache-off** so a golden diff is never served from cache.
- No Python module is deleted until its TS replacement covers historical bug fixtures (not just happy-path) and CI usage.
- No agent-runtime dependency (that is Part 2, #1707); no monorepo orchestrator on day one (Nx never; Turborepo only on a measured trigger).

## Type

AFK

## Blocked by

- Blocked by #1718
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
- Port resolve_policy, PolicyResult, ENV_BYPASS, disclosure_line, resolve_wip_cap, and count_vbrief_wip from scripts/policy.py into packages/core/src/policy/ TypeScript modules.
- Add a thin packages/cli/src/policy.ts CLI plus a policy-parity.ts harness that golden-diffs the TS output against the Python oracle with cache off, and cover the resolution branches with vitest fixtures.
narratives.UserStory: As a deft maintainer, I want the policy surface ported to TypeScript, so that policy resolution no longer depends on the Python runtime.

items.title: policy:show on TypeScript returns the same resolved source a

narrative.Acceptance: policy:show on TypeScript returns the same resolved source and field values as the Python oracle for typed, legacy, and default-fail-closed states.

items.title: disclosure_line renders byte-identical phrasing to scripts/p

narrative.Acceptance: disclosure_line renders byte-identical phrasing to scripts/policy.py for both enabled and disabled branch-policy states.

items.title: policy:allow-direct-commits records an audit row and persist

narrative.Acceptance: policy:allow-direct-commits records an audit row and persists the typed flag, and the parity harness exits 0 against the Python oracle with cache off.

references.title: Issue #1722: feat(ts-migration): port policy surface (show / allow-direct-commits / disclosure) to TS (Wave 2)

expected_outputs: policy-parity exits 0 with byte-identical disclosure_line and resolution output versus the Python oracle (cache off)
vitest coverage >=85% across packages/core/src/policy and packages/cli/src/policy.ts
swarm.missing_traces_justification: Traced to the Python oracle scripts/policy.py via cache-off golden-diff parity fixtures, not a spec section.
